metadata.title: Why the Drug Reached the Tumor and the Tumor Still Grew Back
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Nanomedicine candidate 06. Source: cancer-nanomedicine/chapters/02-tumor-transport-barriers.md (outward interstitial fluid pressure mechanism and inside-out regrowth only).
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = drug / particles accumulating at the rim; CRIMSON = unreached core / outward pressure / regrowth; GOLD = the pressure gradient highlight
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: diffusion limit derivation (1-2mm threshold), four-barrier list, 30-vs-150nm size debate, and radiation resistance. Keep ONLY: the outward-pressure mechanism (leaky vessels + no lymphatics -> fluid buildup -> net outward flow -> particles pushed back to rim) and the inside-out regrowth payoff (hypoxic core survives, selection for resistant cells, tumor regrows from core).
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A nanoparticle drug reaches a mouse tumor. Leaky vessels — particles accumulate. The outer rim clears. Weeks later the tumor doubles in volume, growing from the inside out.
MRI at week three: a two-millimeter viable core. Whole-organ measurement confirmed drug accumulation. The core is untouched. The core then expands.

shot.image_description: An MRI slice of a mouse tumor rendered in editorial flat-print style on aged newsprint. The outer rim of the tumor is dark, indicating drug-killed tissue. A small, lighter circular core in the center remains viable. An annotation ring in crimson circles the core. Camera pushes in slowly toward the core.

props.headline: MRI shows a two-millimeter viable core at week three. The drug…

beats.narration_text: Drug reached the tumor. Vessels were leaky. Particles accumulated. Outer rim cleared. Weeks later the tumor regrows from the core. The drug was present. What kept it out of the core?

The standard account: leaky vessels allow particle extravasation; particles then diffuse inward through tissue. That is accurate as far as it goes. The same leaky vessels produce a second effect that works against inward transport.
Leaky vessel walls flood the tumor interstitium faster than lymphatics can drain it. Tumor lymphatics are absent or non-functional. Fluid accumulates. Interstitial pressure rises.

Fluid flows down pressure gradients. The tumor core reaches pressures five to ten times above normal tissue. Net interstitial flow is outward — from the pressurized core toward the tumor periphery. A particle moving inward is moving against that current.

Particles extravasate near rim vessels. The outward current returns them to the periphery. They accumulate at the rim. The core remains particle-free — not because the extravasation mechanism failed, but because the pressure it created opposes inward transport.

The unreached core is also hypoxic. Oxygen deprivation selects for cells with stress tolerance, suppressed apoptosis, and resistance to cytotoxic agents. The cells the drug never reached are not a random subset — they are the highest-resistance survivors.
The rim cells the drug killed were not the primary recurrence risk. The core cells it never reached were — and months of hypoxic pressure had further selected them for treatment resistance.

Illustrative scenario: a nanoparticle therapy reduces mouse tumor rim volume by sixty percent over three weeks. At week three, core IFP measures twenty-five millimeters of mercury; rim IFP: five. A viable two-millimeter core sits behind a pressure gradient the particles cannot cross. By week eight, tumor volume has doubled, originating from the core.

Drug reached the tumor. The same leaky vessels that allowed accumulation flooded the interstitium and drove net outward flow. Particles remained at the rim. The core survived, selected for resistance, and drove recurrence. Accumulation was confirmed. Core delivery was not achieved.
